description: General-purpose, literature-backed knowledge base for interpreting soil/rhizosphere microbiome abundances. For notable taxa it records ecological role, what drives them up or down, what high/low means, candidate interventions, and citations. Used by scripts/taxon_insights.py to annotate high/low taxa detected in any amplicon dataset (not limited to peanut).
disclaimer: These are GENERAL, literature-based candidate explanations to aid interpretation. They are NOT automated causal proof for a specific soil/dataset. Drivers are context-dependent; always confirm against the cited studies and your own metadata (pH, salinity, fertiliser, etc.).

factors.summary: Often the single strongest driver of bacterial community composition. Acidobacteria/Chloroflyi shift strongly along pH; near-neutral pH usually maximises bacterial diversity.

references.citation: Rousk et al. Soil bacterial community structure along a pH gradient (Craibstone). FEMS Microbiol Ecol
Fertilization shapes bacterial community structure by alteration of soil pH. Front. Microbiol. 2017

factors.summary: N addition favours fast-growing copiotrophs (Proteobacteria, Firmicutes, Bacteroidota) and suppresses oligotrophs (Acidobacteria, Chloroflexi, Verrucomicrobiota); high mineral N also downregulates symbiotic N fixation (Bradyrhizobium/diazotrophs).

references.citation: Soil bacterial community altered by nutrient availability and pH after long-term nutrient addition. Front. Microbiol. 2024
Long-term N fertilization and Bradyrhizobium inoculation on diazotroph community. 2024

factors.summary: Salt stress reduces sensitive taxa and diversity while enriching halotolerant groups (many Bacillus, Halomonas, some Proteobacteria). Halotolerant PGPR and AMF inoculation can partially restore beneficial community structure and yield.

references.citation: AMF increased peanut yield by changing rhizosphere microbial community in saline-alkali soil. 2023
Halotolerant phosphate-solubilising Bacillus altitudinis AD13-4 improves saline-alkali rhizosphere. 2024

factors.summary: Higher labile carbon (compost, residues, root exudates) favours copiotrophs and many biocontrol PGPR; low-carbon soils favour oligotrophs.

references.citation: Copiotroph vs oligotroph life-history classification of soil bacteria (review context).

factors.summary: Crop diversification and legume intercropping change rhizodeposition (flavonoids, coumarins), often increasing beneficial N-fixers and PGPR relative to monoculture.

references.citation: Legume rhizodeposition promotes nitrogen fixation under crop diversification. Nat. Commun. 2024
Intercropping quinoa cultivars changes peanut rhizosphere microbes and yield in saline-alkali soil.

taxa.role: Forms root nodules on legumes and fixes atmospheric N2 into plant-available nitrogen.
taxa.high_when: Legume hosts present, low mineral-N soils, intercropping/diversification that raises flavonoid signalling, after rhizobial inoculation.
taxa.low_when: High mineral nitrogen fertiliser (suppresses nodulation), non-legume systems, very saline or acidic soils.
taxa.implication: High = strong biological N supply and reduced fertiliser need. Low in a legume system = poor nodulation / N-fixation.
taxa.interventions: Inoculate with compatible Bradyrhizobium; reduce mineral-N inputs; use legume intercropping/rotation; correct extreme pH.

references.citation: Long-term N fertilization and Bradyrhizobium inoculation on diazotroph community structure. 2024
Maize-peanut intercropping increases Bradyrhizobium via flavonoids. Front. Plant Sci. 2022

taxa.role: Legume-nodulating N-fixer; some strains also promote growth of non-legumes.
taxa.high_when: Legume hosts, low-N soils, inoculation, cooperative fungal-bacterial assembly.
taxa.implication: High = active symbiotic N fixation potential.
taxa.interventions: Inoculation; reduce N fertiliser; manage pH.

references.citation: AMF-microbe co-occurrence networks show positive Rhizobium associations. Mycorrhiza 2026

taxa.role: Associative diazotroph; produces phytohormones (auxins) that stimulate root growth.
taxa.high_when: Root exudate-rich rhizosphere, moderate moisture, reduced-tillage.
taxa.implication: High = growth-promotion and associative N contribution.
taxa.interventions: Biofertiliser inoculation; organic amendments; conservation tillage.

references.citation: Legume rhizodeposition promotes N fixation by soil microbiota. Nat. Commun. 2024

taxa.role: Plant-growth-promoting rhizobacteria; suppress soil-borne pathogens via siderophores, antibiotics (2,4-DAPG) and induced systemic resistance.
taxa.high_when: Carbon-rich rhizosphere, moderate moisture, after biocontrol inoculation.
taxa.low_when: Low organic carbon, drought, fumigation/strong disturbance.
taxa.implication: High = disease-suppressive potential and growth promotion. Low = weaker natural biocontrol.
taxa.interventions: Inoculate biocontrol Pseudomonas; add organic matter; avoid broad-spectrum fumigants.

references.citation: Fluorescent Pseudomonas biocontrol of Fusarium (siderophore/antibiotic mechanisms).

taxa.role: Spore-forming PGPR; solubilise phosphate, produce lipopeptides (iturin, fengycin, surfactin), tolerate salt/heat; biocontrol of fungal pathogens including aflatoxigenic Aspergillus.
taxa.high_when: Saline/alkaline or stressed soils (halotolerant), organic amendments, after inoculation.
taxa.implication: High = stress resilience, P solubilisation and biocontrol capacity. Useful marker of a suppressive, stress-tolerant community.
taxa.interventions: Inoculate Bacillus biofertiliser/biocontrol; add compost; use for saline-alkali remediation.

references.citation: Halotolerant Bacillus altitudinis AD13-4 enhances saline-alkali tolerance and rhizosphere community. 2024
Bacillus co-inoculation induces resistance to fungal pathogens in legumes.

taxa.role: Prolific antibiotic producers; suppress soil-borne pathogens and can enhance mycorrhization and nutrient cycling.
taxa.interventions: Organic amendments; improve drainage/aeration; actinomycete inoculants.

references.citation: AMF and co-occurring Streptomyces networks in rhizosphere/bulk soil. PMC8914178

taxa.role: Symbiotic fungus that extends hyphal networks improving P/water uptake and stress tolerance.
taxa.high_when: Low-P soils, reduced tillage, mycorrhizal hosts, AMF inoculation.
taxa.low_when: High phosphorus fertiliser, intensive tillage, fungicides, severe salinity.
taxa.implication: High = strong mycorrhizal support (nutrients, stress buffering, yield). Low = missed symbiotic benefit.
taxa.interventions: AMF inoculation; reduce P fertiliser and tillage; avoid unnecessary fungicides.

references.citation: AMF regulate rhizosphere microbiome to alleviate salinity and enhance yield. Front. Microbiol. 2025

taxa.role: Widely used AMF (e.g. R. irregularis) improving nutrient uptake and abiotic-stress tolerance.
taxa.high_when: Low-P, host present, inoculation, saline soils where AMF is selected for.
taxa.implication: High = active mycorrhizal symbiosis and stress alleviation.
taxa.interventions: AMF inoculation; lower P inputs; conservation tillage.

references.citation: The multifaceted roles of AMF in plant responses to salt, drought and cold.

taxa.role: Mycoparasitic fungus; antagonises pathogens (e.g. Aspergillus, Fusarium) and promotes plant growth.
taxa.high_when: Organic-rich, well-aerated soils; after biocontrol application.
taxa.implication: High = strong fungal biocontrol capacity (e.g. against aflatoxigenic fungi).
taxa.interventions: Apply Trichoderma bioinoculants; add organic matter.

references.citation: Trichoderma biocontrol of Aspergillus flavus / aflatoxin (review literature).

taxa.role: Includes Aspergillus flavus, which produces carcinogenic aflatoxins on crops such as peanut and maize.
taxa.high_when: Drought/heat stress, damaged tissue, warm storage, low competitor/biocontrol load.
taxa.low_when: Healthy competitive microbiome, adequate moisture, biocontrol (Trichoderma/Bacillus), proper drying/storage.
taxa.implication: High = elevated spoilage and aflatoxin risk — a food-safety red flag.
taxa.interventions: Biocontrol (atoxigenic strains, Trichoderma, Bacillus); irrigation to avoid drought stress; rapid drying and cool storage.

references.citation: Biocontrol of A. flavus and aflatoxin by antagonistic microbes (review).

taxa.role: Soil-borne fungal genus causing wilts and root rots in many crops.
taxa.low_when: Suppressive soils rich in Pseudomonas/Bacillus/Trichoderma, crop rotation.
taxa.interventions: Rotation; biocontrol (Pseudomonas, Trichoderma); resistant cultivars; improve soil suppressiveness.

references.citation: Suppression of Fusarium by fluorescent Pseudomonas in suppressive soils.

taxa.role: Slow-growing oligotrophs important in acidic/low-nutrient soils and carbon cycling.
taxa.implication: High = low-disturbance, low-nutrient conditions. A drop often signals nutrient enrichment/intensification.
taxa.interventions: If undesired shift: moderate fertiliser inputs; maintain organic matter and stable pH.

references.citation: N addition inhibits oligotrophs (Acidobacteria) and favours copiotrophs. Front. Microbiol. 2024

taxa.role: Very broad phylum; many fast-growing copiotrophs including numerous PGPR and some pathogens.
taxa.implication: High relative abundance is common in fertile/rhizosphere soils; interpret at genus level for specifics.
taxa.interventions: Interpret finer (genus); manage nutrients to balance copiotroph/oligotroph ratio.

references.citation: Nutrient addition favours copiotrophic Proteobacteria. Front. Microbiol. 2024

taxa.role: Decomposers and antibiotic producers (incl. Streptomyces); contribute to suppressiveness and C cycling.
taxa.implication: High = active decomposition and disease-suppressive potential.

references.citation: Copiotroph classification incl. Actinobacteria (review context).

taxa.role: Slow-growing oligotrophs common in low-nutrient and deeper soils.
taxa.interventions: Maintain organic matter; avoid over-fertilisation if diversity is the goal.

references.citation: N addition inhibits oligotrophs incl. Chloroflexi. Front. Microbiol. 2024

taxa.role: Salt-adapted bacteria that thrive in saline environments.
taxa.implication: High = strong salinity signal in the soil; a marker of salt stress.
taxa.interventions: Manage salinity (leaching, gypsum for alkalinity, salt-tolerant crops); use halotolerant PGPR/AMF to support the crop.

references.citation: Halotolerant microbes and saline-alkali rhizosphere management.

taxa.role: Nitrite- (and comammox) oxidising bacteria central to nitrification.
taxa.high_when: Aerobic soils with ammonium supply, moderate pH.
taxa.low_when: Anoxic/waterlogged soils, nitrification inhibitors, very low pH.
taxa.implication: High = active nitrification (ammonium -> nitrate), which affects N availability and potential leaching.
taxa.interventions: Use nitrification inhibitors to reduce N loss if leaching is a concern; manage aeration/moisture.

references.citation: Fertilisation and pH shape nitrifier communities. Front. Microbiol. 2017

taxa.role: Metabolically versatile bacteria; several strains promote growth and tolerate abiotic stress and degrade pollutants.
taxa.interventions: Support with organic matter; relevant for stress/pollution remediation contexts.

references.citation: Rhizosphere PGPR and stress tolerance (saline-alkali context).
